findings.title: Suspicious Process: cmd.exe
findings.description: Process cmd.exe (PID 3908) has anomalous parent-child relationship
findings.confidence_explanation: 7 pieces of supporting evidence identified. Additional evidence required: Network connection correlation, DLL injection verification, Disk execution correlation.
relationship_reasoning: Process anomalies suggest unauthorized execution or masquerading.
DLL or Network anomalies in this process would significantly strengthen the injection hypothesis.
findings.hypothesis: Possible code injection or malicious process masquerading

findings.title: Code Injection: csrss.exe (PID 924)
findings.description: Injected code detected in csrss.exe (PID 924) at 0x850000 — protection: Vad
findings.confidence_explanation: 3 pieces of supporting evidence identified. Additional evidence required: DLL list correlation.

raw_data.reason: Executable memory region with no mapped file (injection indicator)

relationship_reasoning: Executable memory regions with no mapped file are strong indicators of process hollowing or injection.
Correlating with DLL loads can confirm the injected module origin.
findings.confidence_change_reason: 3 pieces of supporting evidence identified. Additional evidence required: DLL list correlation.

findings.title: Prior Art Investigation Assignment via Email
findings.description: Pat McGoo assigned Nitroba prior art investigation to Charlie via email. 8 assignment-related emails found.
supporting_evidence: thunderbird:inbox:Fw: M57.BIZ PRIOR ART INVESTIGATION SERVICES:Tue, 17 Nov 2009 10:30:59 -0800
findings.confidence_explanation: 5 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.

subjects: Fw: M57.BIZ PRIOR ART INVESTIGATION SERVICES

findings.hypothesis: Internal/external email communication related to investigation assignment
findings.confidence_change_reason: 5 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.

findings.title: Suspicious External Email Communications — Potential Exfiltration
findings.description: Charlie sent 6 suspicious emails to external parties including andy@swexpert.com and jamie@project2400.com with subjects: 'I Found Something', 'Instructions', 'Picture'.
findings.confidence_explanation: 6 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.
findings.hypothesis: Communication with external party — potential IP theft exfiltration
findings.confidence_change_reason: 6 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.

findings.title: Quantum Cryptography Document Collection — Data Staging
findings.description: Charlie created a 'Quantum Cryptography' folder containing 11 patent documents. Files include Korean and GB patent filings. Last modified Dec 10-11 2009.
findings.confidence_explanation: 5 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.
findings.hypothesis: Documents collected into folder structure — staging for exfiltration
findings.confidence_change_reason: 5 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.

findings.title: Patent Automation Script — patentauto.py
findings.description: Charlie created and executed patentauto.py — a Python script to automate patent research. LNK file confirms execution on 2009-11-20.
findings.confidence_explanation: 2 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.
findings.hypothesis: Sensitive document accessed or created — potential data staging
findings.confidence_change_reason: 2 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.

findings.title: Nitroba Work Document — Active Investigation File
findings.description: Charlie actively worked on 'Nitroba work.odt' — the primary investigation document. 5 LNK versions found indicating repeated access.
findings.confidence_explanation: 3 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.
findings.hypothesis: Sensitive document accessed or created — potential data staging
findings.confidence_change_reason: 3 pieces of supporting evidence identified. All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.

findings.description: LNK shortcut M57biz.lnk accessed — points to patent business document. Consistent with IP exfiltration scenario.
findings.confidence_explanation: All primary evidence domains correlated successfully.
findings.hypothesis: Sensitive document accessed or created — potential data staging

data.description: Charlie created a 'Quantum Cryptography' folder containing 11 patent documents. Files include Korean and GB patent filings. Last modified Dec 10-11 2009.
Charlie created and executed patentauto.py — a Python script to automate patent research. LNK file confirms execution on 2009-11-20.
Charlie actively worked on 'Nitroba work.odt' — the primary investigation document. 5 LNK versions found indicating repeated access.

Starting with process list — memory is most volatile evidence. Anomalous parent-child relationships indicate process injection or masquerading.
data.expected: Normal Windows process tree with services.exe, svchost.exe hierarchy
data.looking_for: Processes spawned from unexpected parents, known malicious names, orphaned processes

data.description: Process cmd.exe (PID 3908) has anomalous parent-child relationship
Process mdd_1.3.exe (PID 2160) has anomalous parent-child relationship

data.reasoning: Checking network connections after process analysis — if suspicious process has external connection, that is strong C2 indicator.
data.expected: Local connections only for a corporate workstation
data.looking_for: External IPs, unusual ports, connections from suspicious PIDs

data.reasoning: Checking registry Run keys — common persistence mechanism. Malware writes here to survive reboot.
data.expected: Legitimate software entries pointing to Program Files
data.looking_for: Entries pointing to temp dirs, encoded paths, unusual executables

data.reasoning: Running malfind after process analysis — code injection leaves executable memory regions with no mapped file. Cross-referencing with suspicious PIDs found earlier.
data.expected: Clean memory regions for all processes
data.looking_for: PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE regions, VAD anomalies, shellcode indicators
